T0320.title: Bank Direct-Scan QR
T0320.definition: A money-laundering method that uses a payment QR code generated by a banking app for direct scan-and-transfer, disguising dirty money as ordinary bank transactions.
T0320.description: Bank direct scanning uses the bank’s proprietary channel, keeping fund flow within the banking system. Fraud rings consider this more “stable” than third-party payment codes because risk controls tend to lag. Money-muling crews use bank direct-scan codes to receive large illicit sums; the payer completes the transfer by scanning the code via a banking app, and the transaction record resembles a normal person-to-person transfer. These codes are often paired with models like “code-to-code” or “code-to-USDT,” converting renminbi into cryptocurrency or foreign exchange to further sever the traceability chain.
T0320.usageExample: Exchange rate ceiling, code-to-code, code-to-USDT, bank direct scan, UnionPay code.
